top_signals.title: ECB’s Lane Sees Second-Round Effects Taking Time to Appear
top_signals.summary: European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane said knock-on effects from higher energy prices will take a while to show up and that policymakers won’t lock themselves into a path for interest rates in the meantime.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: World Bank Approves $750 Million Kenya Loan, Agrees to Back Loan
top_signals.summary: The World Bank approved a long-awaited $750 million loan to Kenya in support of reforms aimed at enhancing good governance and improving public-finance management.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Businesses face up to budget-busting AI bills
top_signals.summary: A shift to usage-based pricing and new models is making companies rethink spending
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Barclays splashes £750m on Canary Wharf base in ‘strong endorsement’ of London
top_signals.summary: Barclays has taken control of its global headquarters based out of Canary Wharf in a £750m deal hailed as a “strong endorsement” of the financial district and the capital itself. The blue-chip lender has entered a long-term leasehold interest agreement with Canary Wharf Group that grants the bank to use the property – based at [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
